title: 5-Axis Laser Cutting System for Cranial Implants Modification
description: Designed and development of a laser cutting robot for intraoperative cranial implant modification.
fullDescription: Design and development of a compact 5‑axis laser cutting system that automatically resizes customized cranial implants for single-stage cranioplasty. The system enables precise three-dimensional trimming of complex cranial or craniofacial implant geometries within a sterile operating-room environment, overcoming the limitations of manual, surgeon-dependent reshaping. Preliminary experimental results demonstrate superior implant fit, with millimeter-scale gaps between the implant and cranial defect, indicating operative duration reductions and improved aesthetic asymmetry.
highlights: Designed and engineered a novel 5‑axis laser cutting machine specifically optimized for intraoperative, single-stage cranioplasty implant modification
Developed a three-dimensional shaping approach that accurately bevels implant edges and accommodates complex cranial or craniofacial geometries beyond the capabilities of conventional laser cutters
Demonstrated, through preliminary experiments, that the system achieved higher-quality implant–skull fit with only millimeter-level gaps, reduced operative duration, and improved cosmetic outcomes

title: SoccerBot: Turtlebot-based Soccer-Playing Robots
description: Designed and built autonomous robots capable of playing soccer game, integrating user input, motion planning, and control.
fullDescription: EN.530.707 Robot System Programming (final project). We designed and built autonomous soccer-playing robots using Turtlebot platforms that can perceive the environment using Kinect, plan its actions by taking input from the user, and execute the movements. The project involved sensor integration, computer vision, and control algorithm development.
highlights: Integrated kinect sensor for environment perception and ball tracking
Designed Qt User Interface to allow users set the kicking direction
Developed control algorithms for robot motion planning, navigation, and execution

title: iECar: Controller Design of BLDC Motor for Electric Vehicles
description: Designed and implemented a controller for a brushless DC motor used in electric vehicles, focusing on efficiency and performance.
fullDescription: This is my college senior project. I designed a controller for BLDC motors on a four‑wheel independently-drive electric vehicle that supports remote control, with the goal to develop autonomous vehicle, as part of an IoT-integrated intelligent transportation system.
highlights: Assisted in assembling a four-wheel independently driven electric vehicle
Developed a multi-MCU control architecture for distributed vehicle control
Closed-loop control of BLDC hub motors

title: Robot Design: Basketball Robots (RBA)
description: Built an autonomous robot basketball system as an undergraduate team project, integrating perception, planning, and coordinated robot control.
fullDescription: This was my college junior year project. This project involved the end‑to‑end design and fabrication of mobile basketball robots for a mini match. I designed and machined the custom mechanical parts, wired the circuit, designed PCBs, and programmed microcontrollers. Simulations and animations were created to validate the design.
highlights: Designed and built mobile basketball robots, including simulation and animation
Gained hands-on manufacturing experience (lathing, milling, boring, wire EDM, and CNC machining) while fabricating robot components
Developed the electronic and control systems by creating circuit schematics, generating PCBs, and programming microcontrollers to achieve the desired motion control
